title: Piquant Smoky Mexican Chiles Grill
description: A spicy, smoky, and adventurous Mexican dish featuring dried leaves of summer savoury, perfect for grilling in a donabe. Low-glycemic-friendly and full of flavor.
instructions: Grill the ancho chilies at 400°F (200°C) for 10 minutes on each side, or until charred and blistered.
Remove the chilies from the grill and let them cool down.
Once the chilies have cooled, remove the stems and seeds and tear them into bite-sized pieces.
In a blender or food processor, combine the chipotle peppers, garlic, cumin, and a pinch of salt. Blend until smooth.
Add the olive oil, lime juice, and a pinch of salt to the blender and blend until well combined.
Add the blended mixture to the torn ancho chilies and stir to combine.
Heat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat and brush with olive oil.
Add the sliced red onion to the grill and cook for 5 minutes, or until it starts to caramelize.
Add the ancho chilies to the grill and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until slightly charred.
Transfer the ancho chilies to a serving platter and top with chopped cilantro and a squeeze of lime juice.
Serve the piquant smoky Mexican chiles grill hot, garnished with additional cilantro if desired.
Garnish with additional lime wedges, if desired.
